description: Create a renderCardEffect utility that parses icon tokens ({vp}, {coin}, {honor}, {bushi}, {shinto}) in translation strings and returns React elements with inline SVG icons. Update all card effect translations in both en.ts and es.ts to use token syntax. Update all rendering components (SeasonCardsModal, SeasonCardsMarket, PlayerCardsModal) to use the new renderer. Fix Piedad card text specifically and rename deck.archway from 'Arco' to 'Tori' in es.ts.
steps: Create src/utils/renderCardEffect.tsx - a utility that takes a translated string and parses tokens like {vp}3, {coin}2, {honor}1, {bushi}, {shinto}. For each token, replace with the corresponding icon component (VPIcon, CoinIcon, HonorIcon, BushiIcon, ShintoIcon from src/components/Icons.tsx) rendered inline at a small size (e.g. 14-16px) followed by the number if present. Return an array of React elements (ReactNode[]). Use a regex like /\{(vp|coin|honor|bushi|shinto)\}(\d*)/g to find tokens.
Update src/i18n/es.ts: Change 'card.effect.sp-piety' to 'Si ganas una Ficha de Provincia en Guerra con un Shinto, ganas {honor}1 y {vp}3.'

Update all card.effect entries in src/i18n/es.ts to use icon tokens: replace references to 'PV' quantities with {vp}N, 'Moneda(s)' quantities with {coin}N, 'Honor' (when gained/lost as a resource) with {honor}, 'Bushi' unit references with {bushi}, 'Shinto' unit references with {shinto}. Be careful: only replace when referring to gaining/losing quantities, not when referring to game concepts like 'Mandato de Cosecha' or 'símbolo de Guerra'. Examples: '2 PV' -> '{vp}2', '3 Monedas' -> '{coin}3', 'gana Honor' -> 'gana {honor}', 'pierde Honor' -> 'pierde {honor}'.

Update all card.effect entries in src/i18n/en.ts similarly: 'VP' quantities -> {vp}N, 'Coin(s)' quantities -> {coin}N, 'Honor' resource references -> {honor}, 'Bushi' -> {bushi}, 'Shinto' -> {shinto}. Same caution about not replacing conceptual references.

Update src/components/SeasonCardsModal.tsx: Import renderCardEffect. Replace the two locations where card effects are rendered as plain text: (1) line ~285

{t(getCardEffectKey(card.id))}

should become

{renderCardEffect(t(getCardEffectKey(card.id)))}

, (2) line ~442 {t(getCardEffectKey(zoomedCard.id))} should become {renderCardEffect(t(getCardEffectKey(zoomedCard.id)))}

Update src/components/SeasonCardsMarket.tsx: Import renderCardEffect. Replace
{t(getCardEffectKey(card.id))}
with
{renderCardEffect(t(getCardEffectKey(card.id)))}

Update src/components/PlayerCardsModal.tsx: Import renderCardEffect. Replace

{t(getCardEffectKey(card.id))}

with

{renderCardEffect(t(getCardEffectKey(card.id)))}
Run npx tsc --noEmit and npm run build to verify everything compiles cleanly
acceptance_criteria: npx tsc --noEmit passes with no errors
A new file src/utils/renderCardEffect.tsx exists that exports a function accepting a string and returning React nodes with inline icon SVGs for {vp}, {coin}, {honor}, {bushi}, {shinto} tokens
The Spanish translation for sp-piety reads: 'Si ganas una Ficha de Provincia en Guerra con un Shinto, ganas {honor}1 y {vp}3.'
The Spanish translation for deck.archway is 'Tori'
All card.effect entries in both en.ts and es.ts use {vp}N, {coin}N, {honor}, {bushi}, {shinto} tokens where appropriate
SeasonCardsModal.tsx, SeasonCardsMarket.tsx, and PlayerCardsModal.tsx use renderCardEffect() to render card effects with icons
findings: Pre-existing build errors in BattleBiddingOverlay.tsx and BattlePanel.tsx with tsc -b (strict build mode) but not with tsc --noEmit. These are unrelated to this feature. The project's documented verification (npx tsc --noEmit) passes cleanly.
